tldr.text: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 launches as its most capable public model ever, with a secret Mythos 5 variant reserved for US government cyberdefense partners.
Sarvam becomes India's newest AI unicorn after raising $234 million at a $1.5 billion valuation, led by HCLTech.
Anthropic cut off foreign access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 after a Commerce Department export directive, sparking an 'AI war' alarm in Europe.

categories.name: Tools & Products
categories.blurb: The biggest model launches and product drops across the AI landscape this week.

stories.title: Claude Fable 5 lands — with a locked door behind it
stories.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Fable 5, its most capable publicly available model, topping nearly every benchmark in software engineering, science, and vision tasks. A secret sibling, Claude Mythos 5, ships simultaneously but only to US government cyberdefense partners via Project Glasswing, with safeguards on biology and cybersecurity lifted. Pricing comes in at $10 per million input tokens and $50 per million output — less than half the cost of the previous Mythos Preview.

stories.title: Gemma 4 12B: multimodal AI that fits on a laptop
stories.summary: Google released Gemma 4 12B, an open-weight model (Apache 2.0) that runs locally on just 16GB of VRAM and is the first mid-sized Gemma to natively accept audio input. Its encoder-free architecture pipes vision and audio directly into the LLM backbone, achieving benchmark scores close to the 26B MoE model. Multi-Token Prediction drafters keep latency low for real-world agentic use.

stories.title: Google's May mega-drop: Gemini 3.5, Omni, Googlebook, and more
stories.summary: Google I/O 2026 officially kicked off the 'agentic Gemini era' with Gemini 3.5 for complex multi-step workflows and Gemini Omni for any-input-to-video creation. Hardware arrived too: Fitbit Air, a discreet wellness pebble, and the Googlebook laptop built for Gemini Intelligence with a Magic Pointer and cross-device Android features. Universal Cart now lets users add items across Search, YouTube, Gmail, and Gemini in a single shopping hub.

stories.title: ChatGPT Images 2.0 and a personal finance hub roll out
stories.summary: OpenAI shipped ChatGPT Images 2.0, a new image generation model, alongside a personal finance experience now live for Plus users on web and iOS. GPT-5.5 Instant also received a quality upgrade targeting decision-making, planning, and shopping conversations, rolling out first to Plus and Pro users in the US. A new behind-the-scenes speech-to-text model for dictation quietly replaced the old one across all plans.

categories.name: Startups & Money
categories.blurb: A week of unicorn births, billion-dollar bets, and venture funds reaping their Anthropic rewards.

stories.title: Sarvam: India's freshest AI unicorn at $1.5B
stories.summary: Bengaluru-based Sarvam raised $234 million in a Series B round led by HCLTech, vaulting to a $1.5 billion valuation and joining India's unicorn club. Bessemer Venture Partners joined existing backers Khosla Ventures and Peak XV Partners, and Sarvam is targeting a total raise of $300 million. The company recently open-sourced models at 30B and 105B parameters, positioning itself as a sovereign AI infrastructure play.

stories.title: Menlo Ventures raises $3B — Anthropic made them do it
stories.summary: Menlo Ventures announced a $3 billion fund raise, the largest in its 50-year history, driven by a bet-the-firm $500 million investment in Anthropic's 2024 Series D. That stake is now worth roughly $14 billion, turning white-knuckle risk into the VC story of the year. The new fund signals that early concentrated AI bets are producing generational returns for believers.

stories.title: PhysicsX hits $2.4B to bring AI to jet engines and chips
stories.summary: London-based PhysicsX raised $300 million at a $2.4 billion valuation, led by Singapore's Temasek, to build AI models for manufacturing complex components like jet engines and semiconductors. Nvidia and Applied Materials backed previous rounds and remain investors, underlining the hardware industry's appetite for physics-aware AI. The valuation is more than double its prior round, reflecting surging demand for industrial AI.

stories.title: a16z backs Ornn's $33M compute marketplace
stories.summary: Andreessen Horowitz led a $33 million seed round into Ornn, a marketplace where AI compute power can be bought and sold, marking a16z's first investment in a compute exchange. Galaxy Ventures, Nordstar, and SV Angel also participated, with a16z's crypto arm joining for the decentralised infrastructure angle. The bet reflects growing conviction that GPU capacity is a commodity asset class in its own right.

stories.title: Pramaana Labs pulls $27M to apply formal verification to AI
stories.summary: Pramaana Labs secured $27 million in seed funding led by Khosla Ventures, with Accel, Nexus Venture Partners, and Premji Invest among participants, to bring formal verification techniques to AI systems. The approach aims to mathematically prove AI behavior rather than rely solely on empirical testing, addressing reliability concerns at the frontier. It is a rare bet on foundational AI safety infrastructure over application-layer tooling.

categories.name: The Bigger Picture
categories.blurb: Governments on both sides of the Atlantic are tightening their grip on frontier AI, and Anthropic is caught in the middle.

stories.title: Anthropic kills foreign access to Fable 5 on US order
stories.summary: Anthropic disabled access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for foreign nationals after receiving a Commerce Department export directive citing national security. European observers called it proof of US dominance and the Trump administration's desire for control over frontier AI. The move is being closely watched as a live test of how export-control law intersects with AI safety governance frameworks.

stories.title: Florida sues OpenAI over ChatGPT consumer harms
stories.summary: Florida's Attorney General filed the first state-led civil lawsuit against OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman, alleging the company concealed serious risks of ChatGPT and marketed it deceptively to residents including children. The complaint cites dangerous responses, dependency-inducing design, and data collection from minors under consumer protection law. The suit adds to a growing legal pile-on against the company as regulators seek accountability frameworks outside federal AI legislation.

stories.title: States keep legislating as Congress stalls on federal AI law
stories.summary: Six months after Trump warned states off AI regulation, more states than ever are passing their own laws: Connecticut enacted a sweeping AI framework covering companion chatbots and employment decisions, Louisiana became the 22nd state with a consumer data privacy law, and Colorado revised its landmark AI act to take effect January 2027. A discussion draft of the 'Great American AI Act' aims to preempt state rules for three years but has yet to advance. The FTC, meanwhile, has already settled with three marketing firms over deceptive AI capability claims, proving enforcement is moving with or without new federal legislation.

stories.title: UK watchdog gives publishers opt-out from Google AI summaries
stories.summary: The UK Competition and Markets Authority imposed new conduct requirements on Google Search, giving publishers the right to opt out of having their content used in AI Overviews and to refuse fine-tuning data use. Google must also provide clear attribution and links in AI-generated results, restoring some bargaining power for news organisations. The CMA said it will monitor implementation and consider further measures if needed.

stories.title: Court rules expert AI prompts are discoverable in litigation
stories.summary: A federal magistrate judge in Conservation Law Foundation v. Shell Oil ordered a plaintiff to produce the AI prompts its expert used when preparing her report, treating them as part of her methodology rather than protected drafting material. The ruling is one of the clearest signals yet that AI-assisted expert work opens up new discovery battlegrounds. Litigators are now on notice that every prompt their experts run may end up in opposing counsel's hands.
